issues.blurb: The battery loses capacity with charge cycles and poor storage habits, and range drop is the single most common long-term complaint.
Hub or mid-drive motors and their controllers throw errors, cut out, or need firmware updates that affect power delivery and reliability.
Motor torque accelerates chain, cassette, and chainring wear well beyond a non-assisted bike, so drivetrains need replacing sooner.
The added weight and higher speeds put more demand on the brakes, wearing pads and rotors faster and making braking power critical.
Connectors, displays, and the battery interface can let in moisture, leading to intermittent faults or corrosion if not sealed well.

narrative.headline: What e-bike owners report most
narrative.summary: These are issues commonly reported by e-bike owners as a general reference, not expert-verified data and possibly outdated. Battery health, the motor and firmware, and faster drivetrain wear under power tend to come up most.

issues.title: Battery Degradation & Care
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~28%) — the battery loses capacity with cycles and poor storage; range drop is the top long-term complaint.

issues.title: Motor & Firmware
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~22%) — motors and controllers throw errors, cut out, or need firmware updates.

issues.title: Faster Drivetrain Wear Under Torque
issues.blurb: Often reported (~20%) — motor torque wears chains and cassettes faster than a non-assisted bike.

issues.title: Brakes (Heavier Bike)
issues.blurb: Often reported (~18%) — added weight and speed wear pads and rotors faster, making braking power critical.

issues.title: Water Ingress on Electronics
issues.blurb: Reported (~12%) — connectors, displays, and battery interfaces can let in moisture, causing faults or corrosion.
